compensation_note: ~$9,000/month (~$108K annualized) + housing stipend in high-cost areas
role_summary: Software engineering internship working on products used by billions. Rotations through teams like Google Cloud, Search, Maps, Android, or YouTube. Work on search algorithms, distributed systems, or ML infrastructure.
key_requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent
Proficiency in C++, Java, Python, or JavaScript
Experience with data structures and algorithms
Minimum 2 years of university remaining after internship

suggested_projects.project_ref: Personal GitHub repos
suggested_projects.rationale: Shows practical coding ability beyond coursework

personal_note: Google SWE internship is the gold standard — apply early. Their process typically includes OA + 2 technical rounds.
notes: Opens applications ~July each year for Summer following. Strong OA (coding round) is the first gate.

role_summary: Build the infrastructure and products that connect billions of people. Work across Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp, or Meta AI teams. Write code for production systems handling massive scale.
key_requirements: Bachelor's degree in CS or related field
Experience with at least one programming language
1+ year of university remaining after internship

suggested_projects.project_ref: Full-stack project with React backend/frontend
suggested_projects.rationale: Meta uses React heavily — shows framework familiarity

personal_note: Meta interns get a lot of autonomy. Their internship-to-FTE conversion rate is ~60%+.
notes: CoE (Coding Exercise) online assessment required before interview scheduling.

role_summary: Work on products like iOS, macOS, iCloud, or Apple Silicon. Build the software that powers billions of devices. Contribute to Xcode, Swift, Core ML, or device firmware.
key_requirements: Solid understanding of algorithms and systems

suggested_projects.project_ref: iOS/macOS app or systems-level project
suggested_projects.rationale: Apple values low-level and systems work highly

personal_note: Apple's interview process is known for deep system design questions. They value craftsmanship.
notes: Applications open ~July-August. Strong preference for on-site in Cupertino or Austin.

role_summary: Build large-scale distributed systems for AWS, e-commerce, Alexa, or Prime Video. Work on anything from web services to recommendation engines used by hundreds of millions of customers.
key_requirements: Proficiency in Java, C++, Python, or similar
nice_to_haves: Previous internship on a production team

suggested_projects.project_ref: Distributed system or cloud-based project
suggested_projects.rationale: Amazon's scale is unmatched — show you can think big

personal_note: OA is HackerRank coding. The loop includes 5-6 interviews testing DSA + Leadership Principles.
notes: Hiring manager round after OA. Leadership Principles are critically weighted in evaluation.

role_summary: Work on Azure, Office, Windows, GitHub Copilot, or Xbox. Build tools and platforms used by millions of developers worldwide. Microsoft is known for excellent mentorship and work-life balance.
key_requirements: Proficiency in C#, Java, Python, or JavaScript

suggested_projects.project_ref: Cloud-native or productivity tool project
suggested_projects.rationale: Aligns with Microsoft's core business areas

personal_note: Microsoft has one of the best SWE internship cultures. They value growth mindset (vs. fixed mindset in interviews).
notes: OA is CoderPad-style coding round. Interview focuses on collaboration and systems thinking.

location: Santa Clara, CA / Austin, TX / Remote options vary

role_title: Research Scientist Intern / AI Engineer Intern

personal_note: Scale AI is the 'plumbing' behind most of AI's LLM progress. Fast-growing, critical infrastructure.
notes: Apply via scale.com/careers — look for 'Data Scientist Intern' or 'RLHF Engineer'.

role_summary: Build infrastructure for GPT model training and inference at massive scale. Work on distributed training systems, GPU cluster optimization, or serving infrastructure.

suggested_projects.project_ref: Large-scale distributed system with performance optimization
suggested_projects.rationale: OpenAI's infrastructure is unlike anything else — show you can build at massive scale

personal_note: This is the engineering track (not research). If you love building the systems that make GPT work, this is it.
notes: Apply via openai.com/company/jobs — search 'Software Engineer Intern'.

role_summary: Build AI agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot, Bing Chat, and Windows Copilot. Work on LLM integration, agent orchestration, or productivity AI features.
key_requirements: Understanding of LLM function calling and tool use

suggested_projects.project_ref: AI agent project with tool-use capabilities
suggested_projects.rationale: Directly maps to Microsoft's Copilot ecosystem — show you understand autonomous agents

personal_note: Microsoft is putting AI agents into every product. This internship puts you at the center of that transformation.
notes: Apply via careers.microsoft.com — search 'AI Agent Developer Intern'.

location: Palo Alto / Austin / Fremont / Buffalo, NY
role_summary: Build autonomous driving ML models. Work on perception networks, motion planning, or simulation for Tesla's Full Self-Driving system processing billions of miles.

suggested_projects.rationale: Tesla's FSD is vision-based — show you understand real-time CV pipelines

personal_note: Tesla AI is one of the largest-scale real-world ML deployments in existence. 5M+ vehicles generating training data.

role_summary: Build Claude's AI infrastructure — model training at scale, inference optimization, or safety systems. Work on making frontier models faster, cheaper, and safer.

personal_note: Claude's API is growing fast. This role focuses on making the platform reliable and fast for millions of users.

role_title: Cloud AI Engineer Intern — Vertex AI (Summer 2027)
role_summary: Work on Google Cloud's Vertex AI platform — ML pipelines, AutoML, or enterprise AI services. Build the cloud tools that let companies deploy ML models at scale.

Model registry and experiment tracking tools

compensation_note: $225K annualized base (pro-rated ~$18.75K/week) + discretionary bonus + furnished housing provided
role_summary: Build advanced trading and risk applications — decoders for exchange packet data, normalizers for trading books, compliance tools. Highly competitive quant-tech shop with 30+ years of trading expertise.

Exposure to TCP/IP networking, multi-threaded apps, algorithms
Strong OOP, data structures, and algorithms
Excellent C++, Python, or Java skills

compensation_note: $145K+ annualized minimum (per IL Equal Pay Act) + discretionary bonus

US citizenship mandatory for virtually all positions (ITAR clearance)
